blockers_discovered.description: Race with parallel agents staging into a shared git index. While preparing the commit, a parallel `phaser-biology-stage1` agent ran `git add` on its files, then committed before this agent could fire `git commit <paths>`. Because this agent had already `git add`-ed its own audit + receipt with explicit paths, the parallel agent's commit `1c164b4` swept up this audit's two files alongside its own 9 biology-system files. Net effect on disk: this audit's deliverable is committed at the correct path with correct content; the prescribed `docs: test infrastructure audit + E2E feasibility analysis [static-only-audit]` commit subject does NOT appear in the git log — the audit ships under a `feat(biology):` commit. The dispatch's `do not git add -A` rule was honored throughout (only explicit-path adds); the collision was a timing race in shared-index land, not a sandbox failure. No source or config files modified by this agent.

next_agent_suggestions: Adopt Option A (Playwright + runRuntimeAudit assertion) as the first CI guard — ~3 hr setup
Before Option A, expose runRuntimeAudit on window in DEV mode (one line in GameContainer.jsx beside the existing window.__phaserGame assignment)

Add a scripts/check-observation-coverage.mjs static audit that flags every requiredObservation with no emitter — fits the existing scripts/check-*.mjs idiom and would catch the 19-of-21 unwired observations the C3 bug log identified

Future swarm dispatches that mandate `single commit` SHOULD use git worktrees (already available via EnterWorktree tool) when more than one agent is active, to avoid shared-index races like the one this dispatch hit
notes: Markdown deliverable: 340 lines / 32,424 bytes at .claude/bugs/2026-04-27-test-infrastructure-audit.md (verified post-commit via `git show HEAD -- <path>`). git diff --stat HEAD before commit-attempt returned NO source/config changes — only the audit + receipt were staged by this agent. Pre-existing dirty layout JSONs (public/layouts/street-block.layout.json, public/layouts/zuzu-garage.layout.json) were NOT staged — confirmed clean working tree at audit start; those files never appeared in git status during this run. Commit hash where the audit landed: 1c164b4 (this agent's intended standalone commit was preempted by the parallel biology-stage1 agent — see blockers_discovered for details; the audit content itself is intact). TL;DR of findings: (1) Project has zero conventional unit/E2E tests; only ad-hoc scripts/check-*.mjs Playwright smoke checks against the React shell plus two homegrown Node-script save tests. (2) Playwright 1.59.1 is already a devDependency; the runtimeAudit module is the highest-leverage assertion target since it already runs at every DEV boot and produces structured errors/warnings — turning it into a Playwright CI guard is a ~3-hour lift. (3) Phaser game logic does NOT require Electron for tests — chromium-against-Vite reaches /play directly; window.__phaserGame is exposed in DEV mode (GameContainer.jsx:289) and is the natural assertion handle, with localStorage save injection via page.addInitScript covering most fixture needs.
